seoTitle: Split a Long Form Into Sections in Microsoft Forms
metaDescription: Break a lengthy Microsoft Forms survey into multiple pages using Sections, so respondents aren't overwhelmed by one long scroll.
intro: A long questionnaire crammed onto a single page overwhelms respondents and drives up abandonment rates. Microsoft Forms' Section feature lets you divide a survey into distinct, thematic pages, keeping each screen focused and easier to complete.

steps.heading: Find your breakpoint
steps.body: Scroll to the exact spot in your question list where you want to split the layout into a new page.

steps.heading: Open the extended options
steps.body: Click Add New, then tap the drop-down arrow next to the standard field options to reveal the extended menu.

steps.heading: Insert a Section
steps.body: Select the Section option from the list to inject a new page break at that point.

steps.heading: Title and describe the section
steps.body: Click into the new section header to give it a distinct title, and use the sub-description line for any special instructions.

steps.heading: Build questions inside the section
steps.body: Use the Add New button inside the section block to build targeted questions for that specific page, then preview to click through the pagination.

faq.question: Can I move an entire section, questions and all, at once?
faq.answer: Yes, the section ellipsis menu lets you duplicate or move the whole group block up or down within your form.

faq.question: Does adding sections change how responses are exported?
faq.answer: Sections organize the respondent experience into pages, but exported data is still structured by individual question, not by page.

faq.question: How many sections can a form have?
faq.answer: You can add as many section breaks as needed to organize a long questionnaire into manageable pages.

faq.question: Will each section show a progress indicator to respondents?
faq.answer: Forms' multi-page layout guides respondents through each section sequentially as they complete the survey.
